tldr: Corbado is a passkey/auth provider; Dfns is an MPC wallet-as-a-service — only migrate if your real need is programmable custody plus user identity, not pure consumer login.
why_switch: Need MPC-backed wallet creation tied to user identity, not just authentication
Want institutional-grade key management with policy and approval workflows
Targeting regulated fintech / crypto custody use cases
Looking for SOC 2-aligned key infrastructure rather than a passkey UX layer
Consolidating auth and signing under one programmable platform
what_to_export: Registered passkey / credential metadata (public keys, credential IDs)
Session and login logs for audit continuity
API keys and service credentials inventory
export_method: Use Corbado's admin console and any user/credentials export available in your plan to pull the user list and credential metadata; otherwise request a data export from Corbado support. Save raw passkey public keys and credential IDs — these cannot be re-derived. Consult Corbado's official documentation for the current export endpoints rather than relying on assumed APIs.
import_method: Dfns is wallet-as-a-service, not a drop-in auth provider, so importing means provisioning Dfns end-users and wallets via the Dfns API/SDK and re-binding them to your existing user IDs. Passkeys in Dfns' WebAuthn flow generally must be re-registered per user since private credential material cannot move between vendors. Plan a re-enrollment flow and read the Dfns SDK docs for the supported registration sequence.

concept_mapping.note: Map by stable external user ID; Dfns user objects are tied to wallets, not just login.
Re-register; private keys never leave the authenticator.
Issue your own session after Dfns auth; token semantics differ.
Multi-approval is modeled as policy rules, not just a second factor.
Event names and payloads differ — re-subscribe and re-map handlers.
Provision a new app and re-issue service credentials.

gotchas: Dfns is wallet infrastructure, not a passkey-auth replacement — confirm scope before committing
All passkeys must be re-registered; users will see a re-enrollment prompt
Pricing on Dfns is custom/enterprise — get a quote before migration
Dfns lock-in on key shares and policy model is non-trivial to undo
Compliance work (KYC, AML) still sits on your side
Verify exact API/feature names against Dfns' official docs before coding

email.subject: Moving from Corbado to Dfns — re-enrollment plan
email.body:
Hi team,

We're evaluating a move from Corbado (passkey auth) to Dfns (MPC wallet-as-a-service) because our roadmap now needs programmable custody alongside user identity.

The migration is not a like-for-like swap: Dfns is wallet infrastructure, so users will need to re-register their WebAuthn credentials and we'll provision Dfns end-users tied to our existing user IDs. We'll export users, credential metadata, and audit logs from Corbado, then onboard via the Dfns SDK in a phased rollout.

Expect 3-10 days of engineering plus a re-enrollment flow for end users. I'll confirm pricing and exact API surfaces with Dfns before we cut over.

Thanks
